name: Original San Antone Chili
recipeIngredient: 6 each Cloves of Garlic, minced or 1 tsp.
1 1.5 quarts Beef stock or canned beef broth
recipeInstructions:
1. Fry suet in a large heavy kettle until rendered. Remove and discard the suet.
2. Cut meat into 1/2 inch cubes.
3. Combine flour, salt, and pepper in a paper bag or bowl. Add meat and shake to coat. Set aside the remaining flour.
4. Sear the floured meat in hot fat, stirring to prevent sticking.
5. Add onions and garlic; cook until soft.
6. Add beef stock or broth and bring to a boil. Reduce heat and simmer slowly while preparing peppers.
7. Wash peppers under cold running water. Remove stems and seeds. Place in a saucepan and cover with water. Bring to a boil for 5 minutes. Let steep for 10 minutes. Remove peppers and grind or puree, adding some of the cooking water back into the chili.
8. Add the ground peppers to the meat, cover, and simmer until the meat is tender (2-3 hours).
9. Grind cumin seeds in a mortar and pestle or heavy spoon. Add to the chili.
10. If using chili powder as a substitute for dried peppers, add 4 to 6 tablespoons of chili powder.
11. Mix the saved flour with cold water; stir into the chili and cook until thickened (3 to 5 minutes).
12. Makes about 2 quarts.
